deal_breaker_skills.skill: Strong understanding of Data Structures & Algorithms
Proficiency in at least one language (Java/C++/Python)

data.reasoning_weights: Weights for 'must_have_skills' are higher (7-9) as they are derived from 'Basic Qualifications' and 'Key Skills' crucial for an SDE I role, especially at an Amazon-style company (e.g., DSA, OOP, core programming language proficiency, problem-solving). Weights for 'good_to_have_skills' are lower (3-6) as they come from 'Preferred Qualifications' and indicate beneficial experience or knowledge that is not strictly mandatory for an entry-level engineer. Skills like 'Machine Learning' and 'Big Data' received the lowest weight as they are explicitly mentioned as a 'bonus' or 'exposure'.
data.role_signals: backend, distributed systems, microservices, cloud-based architectures, APIs, scalable systems, performance, reliability, SDLC

data.reasoning_weights: Weight 8 is assigned to skills explicitly listed under 'Basic Qualifications' and core 'Key Skills' which are fundamental technical prerequisites for an SDE I role at Amazon, such as Data Structures & Algorithms, Object-Oriented Programming, proficiency in core programming languages (at least one of Java, C++, Python), problem-solving, and foundational knowledge of operating systems, databases, and system design. Weight 5 is assigned to skills mentioned in 'Preferred Qualifications' or implied by 'Key Responsibilities' that are highly beneficial but not absolute prerequisites for an entry-level SDE I, including experience with specific cloud technologies (AWS), advanced architectural patterns (Microservices, Distributed Systems), development practices (CI/CD), and soft skills like communication.
data.role_signals: backend, distributed systems, cloud, microservices, APIs, scalable systems, software development
